1	"""Common class for dataframe schema objects."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import copy
6	import sys
7	import warnings
8	from pathlib import Path
9	from typing import (
10	    Any,
11	    Generic,
12	    Optional,
13	    TypeVar,
14	    Union,
15	    cast,
16	)
17	
18	from pandera import errors
19	from pandera.api.base.schema import BaseSchema
20	from pandera.api.base.types import CheckList, ParserList, StrictType
21	from pandera.api.checks import Check
22	from pandera.api.hypotheses import Hypothesis
23	from pandera.api.parsers import Parser
24	from pandera.dtypes import DataType, UniqueSettings
25	from pandera.engines import PYDANTIC_V2
26	
27	# if python version is < 3.11, import Self from typing_extensions
28	if sys.version_info < (3, 11):
29	    from typing_extensions import Self
30	else:
31	    from typing import Self
32	
33	
34	TDataObject = TypeVar("TDataObject")
35	
36	
37	if PYDANTIC_V2:
38	    from pydantic import GetCoreSchemaHandler
39	    from pydantic_core import core_schema
40	
41	N_INDENT_SPACES = 4
42	
43	
44	class DataFrameSchema(Generic[TDataObject], BaseSchema):
45	    def __init__(
46	        self,
47	        columns: dict[Any, Any] | None = None,
48	        checks: CheckList | None = None,
49	        parsers: ParserList | None = None,
50	        index=None,
51	        dtype: Any | None = None,
52	        coerce: bool = False,
53	        strict: StrictType = False,
54	        name: str | None = None,
55	        ordered: bool = False,
56	        unique: Union[str, list[str]] | None = None,
57	        report_duplicates: UniqueSettings = "all",
58	        unique_column_names: bool = False,
59	        add_missing_columns: bool = False,
60	        title: str | None = None,
61	        description: str | None = None,
62	        metadata: dict | None = None,
63	        drop_invalid_rows: bool = False,
64	    ) -> None:
65	        """Library-agnostic base class for DataFrameSchema definitions.
66	
67	        :param columns: a dict where keys are column names and values are
68	            Column objects specifying the datatypes and properties of a
69	            particular column.
70	        :type columns: mapping of column names and column schema component.
71	        :param checks: dataframe-wide checks.
72	        :param parsers: dataframe-wide parsers.
73	        :param index: specify the datatypes and properties of the index.
74	        :param dtype: datatype of the dataframe. This overrides the data
75	            types specified in any of the columns. If a string is specified,
76	            then assumes one of the valid pandas string values:
77	            http://pandas.pydata.org/pandas-docs/stable/basics.html#dtypes.
78	        :param coerce: whether or not to coerce all of the columns on
79	            validation. This overrides any coerce setting at the column
80	            or index level. This has no effect on columns where
81	            ``dtype=None``.
82	        :param strict: ensure that all and only the columns defined in the
83	            schema are present in the dataframe. If set to 'filter',
84	            only the columns in the schema will be passed to the validated
85	            dataframe. If set to filter and columns defined in the schema
86	            are not present in the dataframe, will throw an error.
87	        :param name: name of the schema.
88	        :param ordered: whether or not to validate the columns order.
89	        :param unique: a list of columns that should be jointly unique.
90	        :param report_duplicates: how to report unique errors
91	            - `exclude_first`: report all duplicates except first occurrence
92	            - `exclude_last`: report all duplicates except last occurrence
93	            - `all`: (default) report all duplicates
94	        :param unique_column_names: whether or not column names must be unique.
95	        :param add_missing_columns: add missing column names with either default
96	            value, if specified in column schema, or NaN if column is nullable.
97	        :param title: A human-readable label for the schema.
98	        :param description: An arbitrary textual description of the schema.
99	        :param metadata: An optional key-value data.
100	        :param drop_invalid_rows: if True, drop invalid rows on validation.
101	
102	        :raises SchemaInitError: if impossible to build schema from parameters
103	
104	        :examples:
105	
106	        >>> import pandera.pandas as pa
107	        >>>
108	        >>> schema = pa.DataFrameSchema({
109	        ...     "str_column": pa.Column(str),
110	        ...     "float_column": pa.Column(float),
111	        ...     "int_column": pa.Column(int),
112	        ...     "date_column": pa.Column(pa.DateTime),
113	        ... })
114	
115	        Use the pandas API to define checks, which takes a function with
116	        the signature: ``pd.Series -> Union[bool, pd.Series]`` where the
117	        output series contains boolean values.
118	
119	        >>> schema_withchecks = pa.DataFrameSchema({
120	        ...     "probability": pa.Column(
121	        ...         float, pa.Check(lambda s: (s >= 0) & (s <= 1))),
122	        ...
123	        ...     # check that the "category" column contains a few discrete
124	        ...     # values, and the majority of the entries are dogs.
125	        ...     "category": pa.Column(
126	        ...         str, [
127	        ...             pa.Check(lambda s: s.isin(["dog", "cat", "duck"])),
128	        ...             pa.Check(lambda s: (s == "dog").mean() > 0.5),
129	        ...         ]),
130	        ... })
131	
132	        See :ref:`here<DataFrameSchemas>` for more usage details.
133	
134	        """
135	
136	        if columns is None:
137	            columns = {}
138	
139	        _validate_columns(columns)
140	        columns = _columns_renamed(columns)
141	
142	        if checks is None:
143	            checks = []
144	        if isinstance(checks, (Check, Hypothesis)):
145	            checks = [checks]
146	
147	        if parsers is None:
148	            parsers = []
149	        if isinstance(parsers, Parser):
150	            parsers = [parsers]
151	
152	        self._dtype: DataType | None = None
153	
154	        super().__init__(
155	            dtype=dtype,
156	            checks=checks,
157	            parsers=parsers,
158	            name=name,
159	            title=title,
160	            description=description,
161	            metadata=metadata,
162	        )
163	
164	        self.columns: dict[Any, Any] = (  # type: ignore[name-defined]
165	            {} if columns is None else columns
166	        )
167	
168	        self.index = index
169	        self.strict: Union[bool, str] = strict
170	        self._coerce = coerce
171	        self.ordered = ordered
172	        self._unique = [unique] if isinstance(unique, str) else unique
173	        self.report_duplicates = report_duplicates
174	        self.unique_column_names = unique_column_names
175	        self.add_missing_columns = add_missing_columns
176	        self.drop_invalid_rows = drop_invalid_rows
177	
178	        self.metadata = metadata
179	
180	        self._validate_attributes()
181	
182	    def _validate_attributes(self):
183	        if self.strict not in (False, True, "filter"):
184	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
185	                "strict parameter must equal either `True`, `False`, or `'filter'`."
186	            )
187	
188	    @property
189	    def dtype(
190	        self,
191	    ) -> DataType:
192	        """Get the dtype property."""
193	        return self._dtype  # type: ignore
194	
195	    @dtype.setter
196	    def dtype(self, value: Any) -> None:
197	        """Set the dtype property."""
198	        raise NotImplementedError
199	
200	    @property
201	    def coerce(self) -> bool:
202	        """Whether to coerce series to specified type."""
203	        if isinstance(self.dtype, DataType):
204	            return self.dtype.auto_coerce or self._coerce
205	        return self._coerce
206	
207	    @coerce.setter
208	    def coerce(self, value: bool) -> None:
209	        """Set coerce attribute"""
210	        self._coerce = value
211	
212	    @property
213	    def unique(self):
214	        """List of columns that should be jointly unique."""
215	        return self._unique
216	
217	    @unique.setter
218	    def unique(self, value: Union[str, list[str]] | None) -> None:
219	        """Set unique attribute."""
220	        self._unique = [value] if isinstance(value, str) else value
221	
222	    @property
223	    def dtypes(self) -> dict[str, DataType]:
224	        """
225	        A dict where the keys are column names and values are
226	        :class:`~pandera.dtypes.DataType` s for the column. Excludes columns
227	        where `regex=True`.
228	
229	        :returns: dictionary of columns and their associated dtypes.
230	        """
231	        regex_columns = [
232	            name for name, col in self.columns.items() if col.regex
233	        ]
234	        if regex_columns:
235	            warnings.warn(
236	                "Schema has columns specified as regex column names: "
237	                f"{regex_columns}. Use the `get_dtypes` to get the datatypes "
238	                "for these columns.",
239	                UserWarning,
240	            )
241	        return {n: c.dtype for n, c in self.columns.items() if not c.regex}
242	
243	    def get_metadata(self) -> dict | None:
244	        """Provide metadata for columns and schema level"""
245	        res: dict[Any, Any] = {"columns": {}}
246	        for k in self.columns.keys():
247	            res["columns"][k] = self.columns[k].properties["metadata"]
248	
249	        res["dataframe"] = self.metadata
250	
251	        meta = {}
252	        meta[self.name] = res
253	        return meta
254	
255	    def get_dtypes(self, check_obj: TDataObject) -> dict[str, DataType]:
256	        """
257	        Same as the ``dtype`` property, but expands columns where
258	        ``regex == True`` based on the supplied dataframe.
259	
260	        :returns: dictionary of columns and their associated dtypes.
261	        """
262	        regex_dtype = {}
263	        for _, column in self.columns.items():
264	            backend = column.get_backend(check_obj)
265	            if column.regex:
266	                regex_dtype.update(
267	                    {
268	                        c: column.dtype
269	                        for c in backend.get_regex_columns(column, check_obj)
270	                    }
271	                )
272	        return {
273	            **{n: c.dtype for n, c in self.columns.items() if not c.regex},
274	            **regex_dtype,
275	        }
276	
277	    def coerce_dtype(self, check_obj: TDataObject) -> TDataObject:
278	        return self.get_backend(check_obj).coerce_dtype(check_obj, schema=self)
279	
280	    def __call__(
281	        self,
282	        dataframe: TDataObject,
283	        head: int | None = None,
284	        tail: int | None = None,
285	        sample: int | None = None,
286	        random_state: int | None = None,
287	        lazy: bool = False,
288	        inplace: bool = False,
289	    ) -> TDataObject:
290	        """Alias for :func:`DataFrameSchema.validate` method.
291	
292	        :param pd.DataFrame dataframe: the dataframe to be validated.
293	        :param head: validate the first n rows. Rows overlapping with `tail` or
294	            `sample` are de-duplicated.
295	        :type head: int
296	        :param tail: validate the last n rows. Rows overlapping with `head` or
297	            `sample` are de-duplicated.
298	        :type tail: int
299	        :param sample: validate a random sample of n rows. Rows overlapping
300	            with `head` or `tail` are de-duplicated.
301	        :param random_state: random seed for the ``sample`` argument.
302	        :param lazy: if True, lazily evaluates dataframe against all validation
303	            checks and raises a ``SchemaErrors``. Otherwise, raise
304	            ``SchemaError`` as soon as one occurs.
305	        :param inplace: if True, applies coercion to the object of validation,
306	            otherwise creates a copy of the data.
307	        """
308	        return self.validate(
309	            dataframe, head, tail, sample, random_state, lazy, inplace
310	        )
311	
312	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
313	        """Represent string for logging."""
314	        return (
315	            f"<Schema {self.__class__.__name__}("
316	            f"columns={self.columns}, "
317	            f"checks={self.checks}, "
318	            f"parsers={self.parsers}, "
319	            f"index={self.index.__repr__()}, "
320	            f"dtype={self._dtype}, "
321	            f"coerce={self.coerce}, "
322	            f"strict={self.strict}, "
323	            f"name={self.name}, "
324	            f"ordered={self.ordered}, "
325	            f"unique={self.unique}, "
326	            f"report_duplicates={self.report_duplicates}, "
327	            f"unique_column_names={self.unique_column_names}, "
328	            f"add_missing_columns={self.add_missing_columns}, "
329	            f"title={self.title}, "
330	            f"description={self.description}, "
331	            f"metadata={self.metadata}, "
332	            f"drop_invalid_rows={self.drop_invalid_rows}"
333	            ")>"
334	        )
335	
336	    def __str__(self) -> str:
337	        """Represent string for user inspection."""
338	
339	        def _format_multiline(json_str, arg):
340	            return "\n".join(
341	                f"{indent}{line}" if i != 0 else f"{indent}{arg}={line}"
342	                for i, line in enumerate(json_str.split("\n"))
343	            )
344	
345	        indent = " " * N_INDENT_SPACES
346	        if self.columns:
347	            columns_str = f"{indent}columns={{\n"
348	            for colname, col in self.columns.items():
349	                columns_str += f"{indent * 2}'{colname}': {col}\n"
350	            columns_str += f"{indent}}}"
351	        else:
352	            columns_str = f"{indent}columns={{}}"
353	
354	        if self.checks:
355	            checks_str = f"{indent}checks=[\n"
356	            for check in self.checks:
357	                checks_str += f"{indent * 2}{check}\n"
358	            checks_str += f"{indent}]"
359	        else:
360	            checks_str = f"{indent}checks=[]"
361	
362	        if self.parsers:
363	            parsers_str = f"{indent}parsers=[\n"
364	            for parser in self.parsers:
365	                parsers_str += f"{indent * 2}{parser}\n"
366	            parsers_str += f"{indent}]"
367	        else:
368	            parsers_str = f"{indent}parsers=[]"
369	
370	        # add additional indents
371	        index_ = str(self.index).split("\n")
372	        if len(index_) == 1:
373	            index = str(self.index)
374	        else:
375	            index = "\n".join(
376	                x if i == 0 else f"{indent}{x}" for i, x in enumerate(index_)
377	            )
378	
379	        return (
380	            f"<Schema {self.__class__.__name__}(\n"
381	            f"{columns_str},\n"
382	            f"{checks_str},\n"
383	            f"{parsers_str},\n"
384	            f"{indent}coerce={self.coerce},\n"
385	            f"{indent}dtype={self._dtype},\n"
386	            f"{indent}index={index},\n"
387	            f"{indent}strict={self.strict},\n"
388	            f"{indent}name={self.name},\n"
389	            f"{indent}ordered={self.ordered},\n"
390	            f"{indent}unique_column_names={self.unique_column_names},\n"
391	            f"{indent}metadata={self.metadata}, \n"
392	            f"{indent}add_missing_columns={self.add_missing_columns}\n"
393	            ")>"
394	        )
395	
396	    def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:
397	        if not isinstance(other, type(self)):
398	            return NotImplemented
399	
400	        return self.__dict__ == other.__dict__
401	
402	    if PYDANTIC_V2:
403	
404	        @classmethod
405	        def __get_pydantic_core_schema__(
406	            cls, _source_type: Any, _handler: GetCoreSchemaHandler
407	        ) -> core_schema.CoreSchema:
408	            return core_schema.no_info_plain_validator_function(
409	                cls._pydantic_validate,
410	            )
411	
412	    else:
413	
414	        @classmethod
415	        def __get_validators__(cls):
416	            yield cls._pydantic_validate
417	
418	    @classmethod
419	    def _pydantic_validate(cls, schema: Any) -> Self:
420	        """Verify that the input is a compatible DataFrameSchema."""
421	        if not isinstance(schema, cls):  # type: ignore
422	            raise TypeError(f"{schema} is not a {cls}.")
423	
424	        return cast(Self, schema)
425	
426	    #################################
427	    # Schema Transformation Methods #
428	    #################################
429	
430	    def add_columns(self, extra_schema_cols: dict[str, Any]) -> Self:
431	        """
432	        Create a copy of the :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
433	        with extra columns.
434	
435	        :param extra_schema_cols: Additional columns of the format
436	        :type extra_schema_cols: DataFrameSchema
437	        :returns: a new :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
438	            with the extra_schema_cols added.
439	
440	        :example:
441	
442	        To add columns to the schema, pass a dictionary with column name and
443	        ``Column`` instance key-value pairs.
444	
445	        >>> import pandera.pandas as pa
446	        >>>
447	        >>> example_schema = pa.DataFrameSchema(
448	        ...    {
449	        ...        "category": pa.Column(str),
450	        ...        "probability": pa.Column(float),
451	        ...    }
452	        ... )
453	        >>> print(
454	        ...     example_schema.add_columns({"even_number": pa.Column(pa.Bool)})
455	        ... )
456	        <Schema DataFrameSchema(
457	            columns={
458	                'category': <Schema Column(name=category, type=DataType(str))>
459	                'probability': <Schema Column(name=probability, type=DataType(float64))>
460	                'even_number': <Schema Column(name=even_number, type=DataType(bool))>
461	            },
462	            checks=[],
463	            parsers=[],
464	            coerce=False,
465	            dtype=None,
466	            index=None,
467	            strict=False,
468	            name=None,
469	            ordered=False,
470	            unique_column_names=False,
471	            metadata=None,
472	            add_missing_columns=False
473	        )>
474	
475	        .. seealso:: :func:`remove_columns`
476	
477	        """
478	        schema_copy = copy.deepcopy(self)
479	        schema_copy.columns = {
480	            **schema_copy.columns,
481	            **self.__class__(extra_schema_cols).columns,
482	        }
483	        return cast(Self, schema_copy)
484	
485	    def remove_columns(self, cols_to_remove: list[str]) -> Self:
486	        """
487	        Removes columns from a :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
488	        and returns a new copy.
489	
490	        :param cols_to_remove: Columns to be removed from the
491	            ``DataFrameSchema``
492	        :type cols_to_remove: List
493	        :returns: a new :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema` without the cols_to_remove
494	        :raises: :class:`~pandera.errors.SchemaInitError`: if column not in
495	            schema.
496	
497	        :example:
498	
499	        To remove a column or set of columns from a schema, pass a list of
500	        columns to be removed:
501	
502	        >>> import pandera.pandas as pa
503	        >>>
504	        >>> example_schema = pa.DataFrameSchema(
505	        ...     {
506	        ...         "category" : pa.Column(str),
507	        ...         "probability": pa.Column(float)
508	        ...     }
509	        ... )
510	        >>>
511	        >>> print(example_schema.remove_columns(["category"]))
512	        <Schema DataFrameSchema(
513	            columns={
514	                'probability': <Schema Column(name=probability, type=DataType(float64))>
515	            },
516	            checks=[],
517	            parsers=[],
518	            coerce=False,
519	            dtype=None,
520	            index=None,
521	            strict=False,
522	            name=None,
523	            ordered=False,
524	            unique_column_names=False,
525	            metadata=None,
526	            add_missing_columns=False
527	        )>
528	
529	        .. seealso:: :func:`add_columns`
530	
531	        """
532	        schema_copy = copy.deepcopy(self)
533	
534	        # ensure all specified keys are present in the columns
535	        not_in_cols: list[str] = [
536	            x for x in cols_to_remove if x not in schema_copy.columns.keys()
537	        ]
538	        if not_in_cols:
539	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
540	                f"Keys {not_in_cols} not found in schema columns!"
541	            )
542	
543	        for col in cols_to_remove:
544	            schema_copy.columns.pop(col)
545	
546	        return cast(Self, schema_copy)
547	
548	    def update_column(self, column_name: str, **kwargs) -> Self:
549	        """
550	        Create copy of a :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
551	        with updated column properties.
552	
553	        :param column_name:
554	        :param kwargs: key-word arguments supplied to
555	            :class:`~pandera.api.pandas.components.Column`
556	        :returns: a new :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema` with updated column
557	        :raises: :class:`~pandera.errors.SchemaInitError`: if column not in
558	            schema or you try to change the name.
559	
560	        :example:
561	
562	        Calling ``schema.1`` returns the :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
563	        with the updated column.
564	
565	        >>> import pandera.pandas as pa
566	        >>>
567	        >>> example_schema = pa.DataFrameSchema({
568	        ...     "category" : pa.Column(str),
569	        ...     "probability": pa.Column(float)
570	        ... })
571	        >>> print(
572	        ...     example_schema.update_column(
573	        ...         'category', dtype=pa.Category
574	        ...     )
575	        ... )
576	        <Schema DataFrameSchema(
577	            columns={
578	                'category': <Schema Column(name=category, type=DataType(category))>
579	                'probability': <Schema Column(name=probability, type=DataType(float64))>
580	            },
581	            checks=[],
582	            parsers=[],
583	            coerce=False,
584	            dtype=None,
585	            index=None,
586	            strict=False,
587	            name=None,
588	            ordered=False,
589	            unique_column_names=False,
590	            metadata=None,
591	            add_missing_columns=False
592	        )>
593	
594	        .. seealso:: :func:`rename_columns`
595	
596	        """
597	        # check that columns exist in schema
598	
599	        schema = self
600	        if "name" in kwargs:
601	            raise ValueError("cannot update 'name' of the column.")
602	        if column_name not in schema.columns:
603	            raise ValueError(f"column '{column_name}' not in {schema}")
604	        schema_copy = copy.deepcopy(schema)
605	        column_copy = copy.deepcopy(schema.columns[column_name]).set_name(
606	            column_name
607	        )
608	        new_column = column_copy.__class__(
609	            **{**column_copy.properties, **kwargs}
610	        )
611	        schema_copy.columns.update({column_name: new_column})
612	        return cast(Self, schema_copy)
613	
614	    def update_columns(
615	        self,
616	        update_dict: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
617	    ) -> Self:
618	        """
619	        Create copy of a :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
620	        with updated column properties.
621	
622	        :param update_dict:
623	        :return: a new :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema` with updated columns
624	        :raises: :class:`~pandera.errors.SchemaInitError`: if column not in
625	            schema or you try to change the name.
626	
627	        :example:
628	
629	        Calling ``schema.update_columns`` returns the
630	        :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
631	        with the updated columns.
632	
633	        >>> import pandera.pandas as pa
634	        >>>
635	        >>> example_schema = pa.DataFrameSchema({
636	        ...     "category" : pa.Column(str),
637	        ...     "probability": pa.Column(float)
638	        ... })
639	        >>>
640	        >>> print(
641	        ...     example_schema.update_columns(
642	        ...         {"category": {"dtype":pa.Category}}
643	        ...     )
644	        ... )
645	        <Schema DataFrameSchema(
646	            columns={
647	                'category': <Schema Column(name=category, type=DataType(category))>
648	                'probability': <Schema Column(name=probability, type=DataType(float64))>
649	            },
650	            checks=[],
651	            parsers=[],
652	            coerce=False,
653	            dtype=None,
654	            index=None,
655	            strict=False,
656	            name=None,
657	            ordered=False,
658	            unique_column_names=False,
659	            metadata=None,
660	            add_missing_columns=False
661	        )>
662	
663	        """
664	
665	        from pandera.api.dataframe.components import ComponentSchema
666	
667	        new_schema = copy.deepcopy(self)
668	
669	        # ensure all specified keys are present in the columns
670	        not_in_cols: list[str] = [
671	            x for x in update_dict.keys() if x not in new_schema.columns.keys()
672	        ]
673	        if not_in_cols:
674	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
675	                f"Keys {not_in_cols} not found in schema columns!"
676	            )
677	
678	        new_columns: dict[str, ComponentSchema] = {}
679	        for col in new_schema.columns:
680	            # check
681	            if update_dict.get(col):
682	                if update_dict[col].get("name"):
683	                    raise errors.SchemaInitError(
684	                        "cannot update 'name' \
685	                                             property of the column."
686	                    )
687	            original_properties = new_schema.columns[col].properties
688	            if update_dict.get(col):
689	                new_properties = copy.deepcopy(original_properties)
690	                new_properties.update(update_dict[col])
691	                new_columns[col] = new_schema.columns[col].__class__(
692	                    **new_properties
693	                )
694	            else:
695	                new_columns[col] = new_schema.columns[col].__class__(
696	                    **original_properties
697	                )
698	
699	        new_schema.columns = new_columns
700	
701	        return cast(Self, new_schema)
702	
703	    def rename_columns(self, rename_dict: dict[str, str]) -> Self:
704	        """Rename columns using a dictionary of key-value pairs.
705	
706	        :param rename_dict: dictionary of 'old_name': 'new_name' key-value
707	            pairs.
708	        :returns: :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema` (copy of original)
709	        :raises: :class:`~pandera.errors.SchemaInitError` if column not in the
710	            schema.
711	
712	        :example:
713	
714	        To rename a column or set of columns, pass a dictionary of old column
715	        names and new column names, similar to the pandas DataFrame method.
716	
717	        >>> import pandera.pandas as pa
718	        >>>
719	        >>> example_schema = pa.DataFrameSchema({
720	        ...     "category" : pa.Column(str),
721	        ...     "probability": pa.Column(float)
722	        ... })
723	        >>>
724	        >>> print(
725	        ...     example_schema.rename_columns({
726	        ...         "category": "categories",
727	        ...         "probability": "probabilities"
728	        ...     })
729	        ... )
730	        <Schema DataFrameSchema(
731	            columns={
732	                'categories': <Schema Column(name=categories, type=DataType(str))>
733	                'probabilities': <Schema Column(name=probabilities, type=DataType(float64))>
734	            },
735	            checks=[],
736	            parsers=[],
737	            coerce=False,
738	            dtype=None,
739	            index=None,
740	            strict=False,
741	            name=None,
742	            ordered=False,
743	            unique_column_names=False,
744	            metadata=None,
745	            add_missing_columns=False
746	        )>
747	
748	        .. seealso:: :func:`update_column`
749	
750	        """
751	        new_schema = copy.deepcopy(self)
752	
753	        # ensure all specified keys are present in the columns
754	        not_in_cols: list[str] = [
755	            x for x in rename_dict.keys() if x not in new_schema.columns.keys()
756	        ]
757	        if not_in_cols:
758	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
759	                f"Keys {not_in_cols} not found in schema columns!"
760	            )
761	
762	        # remove any mapping to itself as this is a no-op
763	        rename_dict = {k: v for k, v in rename_dict.items() if k != v}
764	
765	        # ensure all new keys are not present in the current column names
766	        already_in_columns: list[str] = [
767	            x for x in rename_dict.values() if x in new_schema.columns.keys()
768	        ]
769	        if already_in_columns:
770	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
771	                f"Keys {already_in_columns} already found in schema columns!"
772	            )
773	
774	        # We iterate over the existing columns dict and replace those keys
775	        # that exist in the rename_dict
776	
777	        new_columns = {
778	            (rename_dict[col_name] if col_name in rename_dict else col_name): (
779	                col_attrs.set_name(rename_dict[col_name])
780	                if col_name in rename_dict
781	                else col_attrs
782	            )
783	            for col_name, col_attrs in new_schema.columns.items()
784	        }
785	
786	        new_schema.columns = new_columns
787	        return cast(Self, new_schema)
788	
789	    def update_index(self, index_name: str, **kwargs) -> Self:
790	        """
791	        Create copy of a :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
792	        with updated index properties.
793	
794	        :param index_name:
795	        :param kwargs: key-word arguments supplied to
796	            :class:`~pandera.api.pandas.components.Index`
797	        :returns: a new :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema` with updated index
798	        :raises: :class:`~pandera.errors.SchemaInitError`: if index not in
799	            schema or you try to change the name.
800	
801	        :example:
802	
803	        Calling ``schema.1`` returns the :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
804	        with the updated index.
805	
806	        """
807	
808	        schema = self
809	        if "name" in kwargs:
810	            raise ValueError("cannot update 'name' of the index.")
811	        if schema.index is None:
812	            raise errors.SchemaInitError("index not in schema")
813	        if index_name not in schema.index.names:
814	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
815	                f"index '{index_name}' not in {schema}"
816	            )
817	
818	        tmp_index_cols = schema.index.names
819	        schema = schema.reset_index()
820	        schema = schema.update_column(index_name, **kwargs)
821	        schema = schema.set_index(tmp_index_cols)
822	
823	        return cast(Self, schema)
824	
825	    def update_indexes(self, update_dict: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]) -> Self:
826	        """
827	        Create copy of a :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
828	        with updated index properties.
829	
830	        :param update_dict:
831	        :return: a new :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema` with updated index
832	        :raises: :class:`~pandera.errors.SchemaInitError`: if index not in
833	            schema or you try to change the name.
834	
835	        """
836	        schema = self
837	
838	        if schema.index is None:
839	            raise errors.SchemaInitError("index not in schema")
840	
841	        # ensure all specified keys are present in the index
842	        not_in_cols: list[str] = [
843	            x for x in update_dict.keys() if x not in schema.index.names
844	        ]
845	        if not_in_cols:
846	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
847	                f"Keys {not_in_cols} not found in schema indexes!"
848	            )
849	
850	        tmp_index_cols = schema.index.names
851	        schema = schema.reset_index()
852	        schema = schema.update_columns(update_dict)
853	        schema = schema.set_index(tmp_index_cols)
854	
855	        return cast(Self, schema)
856	
857	    def rename_indexes(self, rename_dict: dict[str, str]) -> Self:
858	        """Rename indexes using a dictionary of key-value pairs.
859	
860	        :param rename_dict: dictionary of 'old_name': 'new_name' key-value
861	            pairs.
862	        :returns: :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema` (copy of original)
863	        :raises: :class:`~pandera.errors.SchemaInitError` if index not in the
864	            schema.
865	
866	        :example:
867	
868	        To rename an index or set of indexes, pass a dictionary of old index
869	        names and new index names, similar to the pandas DataFrame method.
870	
871	        .. seealso:: :func:`update_index`
872	
873	        """
874	        new_schema = copy.deepcopy(self)
875	
876	        if new_schema.index is None:
877	            raise errors.SchemaInitError("index not in schema")
878	
879	        # ensure all specified keys are present in the index
880	        not_in_cols: list[str] = [
881	            x for x in rename_dict.keys() if x not in new_schema.index.names
882	        ]
883	        if not_in_cols:
884	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
885	                f"Keys {not_in_cols} not found in schema indexes!"
886	            )
887	
888	        tmp_index_cols = new_schema.index.names
889	        new_schema = new_schema.reset_index()
890	
891	        new_schema = new_schema.rename_columns(rename_dict)
892	
893	        new_schema = new_schema.set_index(
894	            # Use the new name in the rename_dict if it exists
895	            # otherwise use the original name
896	            [cast(str, rename_dict.get(n, n)) for n in tmp_index_cols]
897	        )
898	
899	        return cast(Self, new_schema)
900	
901	    def select_columns(self, columns: list[Any]) -> Self:
902	        """Select subset of columns in the schema.
903	
904	        *New in version 0.4.5*
905	
906	        :param columns: list of column names to select.
907	        :returns: :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
908	            (copy of original) with only the selected columns, in the order specified.
909	        :raises: :class:`~pandera.errors.SchemaInitError` if column not in the
910	            schema.
911	
912	        :example:
913	
914	        To subset and reorder a schema by column, and return a new schema:
915	
916	        >>> import pandera.pandas as pa
917	        >>>
918	        >>> example_schema = pa.DataFrameSchema({
919	        ...     "category": pa.Column(str),
920	        ...     "probability": pa.Column(float),
921	        ...     "timestamp": pa.Column(pa.DateTime)
922	        ... })
923	        >>>
924	        >>> print(example_schema.select_columns(['probability', 'category']))
925	        <Schema DataFrameSchema(
926	            columns={
927	                'probability': <Schema Column(name=probability, type=DataType(float64))>
928	                'category': <Schema Column(name=category, type=DataType(str))>
929	            },
930	            checks=[],
931	            parsers=[],
932	            coerce=False,
933	            dtype=None,
934	            index=None,
935	            strict=False,
936	            name=None,
937	            ordered=False,
938	            unique_column_names=False,
939	            metadata=None,
940	            add_missing_columns=False
941	        )>
942	
943	        .. note:: If an index is present in the schema, it will also be
944	            included in the new schema. The columns will be reordered
945	            to match the order in ``columns``.
946	        """
947	        new_schema = copy.deepcopy(self)
948	        # ensure all specified keys are present in the columns
949	        not_in_cols: list[str] = [
950	            x for x in columns if x not in new_schema.columns.keys()
951	        ]
952	        if not_in_cols:
953	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
954	                f"Keys {not_in_cols} not found in schema columns!"
955	            )
956	
957	        new_columns = {
958	            col_name: new_schema.columns[col_name] for col_name in columns
959	        }
960	
961	        new_schema.columns = new_columns
962	        return cast(Self, new_schema)
963	
964	    def set_index(
965	        self, keys: list[str], drop: bool = True, append: bool = False
966	    ) -> Self:
967	        """
968	        A method for setting the :class:`Index` of a
969	        :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`,
970	        via an existing :class:`Column` or list of columns.
971	
972	        :param keys: list of labels
973	        :param drop: bool, default True
974	        :param append: bool, default False
975	        :return: a new :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema` with specified column(s) in the
976	            index.
977	        :raises: :class:`~pandera.errors.SchemaInitError` if column not in the
978	            schema.
979	
980	        :examples:
981	
982	        Just as you would set the index in a ``pandas`` DataFrame from an
983	        existing column, you can set an index within the schema from an
984	        existing column in the schema.
985	
986	        >>> import pandera.pandas as pa
987	        >>>
988	        >>> example_schema = pa.DataFrameSchema({
989	        ...     "category" : pa.Column(str),
990	        ...     "probability": pa.Column(float)})
991	        >>>
992	        >>> print(example_schema.set_index(['category']))
993	        <Schema DataFrameSchema(
994	            columns={
995	                'probability': <Schema Column(name=probability, type=DataType(float64))>
996	            },
997	            checks=[],
998	            parsers=[],
999	            coerce=False,
1000	            dtype=None,
1001	            index=<Schema Index(name=category, type=DataType(str))>,
1002	            strict=False,
1003	            name=None,
1004	            ordered=False,
1005	            unique_column_names=False,
1006	            metadata=None,
1007	            add_missing_columns=False
1008	        )>
1009	
1010	        If you have an existing index in your schema, and you would like to
1011	        append a new column as an index to it (yielding a :class:`Multiindex`),
1012	        just use set_index as you would in pandas.
1013	
1014	        >>> example_schema = pa.DataFrameSchema(
1015	        ...     {
1016	        ...         "column1": pa.Column(str),
1017	        ...         "column2": pa.Column(int)
1018	        ...     },
1019	        ...     index=pa.Index(name = "column3", dtype = int)
1020	        ... )
1021	        >>>
1022	        >>> print(example_schema.set_index(["column2"], append = True))
1023	        <Schema DataFrameSchema(
1024	            columns={
1025	                'column1': <Schema Column(name=column1, type=DataType(str))>
1026	            },
1027	            checks=[],
1028	            parsers=[],
1029	            coerce=False,
1030	            dtype=None,
1031	            index=<Schema MultiIndex(
1032	                indexes=[
1033	                    <Schema Index(name=column3, type=DataType(int64))>
1034	                    <Schema Index(name=column2, type=DataType(int64))>
1035	                ]
1036	                coerce=False,
1037	                strict=False,
1038	                name=None,
1039	                ordered=True
1040	            )>,
1041	            strict=False,
1042	            name=None,
1043	            ordered=False,
1044	            unique_column_names=False,
1045	            metadata=None,
1046	            add_missing_columns=False
1047	        )>
1048	
1049	        .. seealso:: :func:`reset_index`
1050	
1051	        """
1052	
1053	        from pandera.api.pandas.components import Index, MultiIndex
1054	
1055	        new_schema = copy.deepcopy(self)
1056	
1057	        keys_temp: list = (
1058	            list(set(keys)) if not isinstance(keys, list) else keys
1059	        )
1060	
1061	        # ensure all specified keys are present in the columns
1062	        not_in_cols: list[str] = [
1063	            x for x in keys_temp if x not in new_schema.columns.keys()
1064	        ]
1065	        if not_in_cols:
1066	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
1067	                f"Keys {not_in_cols} not found in schema columns!"
1068	            )
1069	
1070	        # if there is already an index, append or replace according to
1071	        # parameters
1072	        ind_list: list = (
1073	            []
1074	            if new_schema.index is None or not append
1075	            else (
1076	                list(new_schema.index.indexes)
1077	                if isinstance(new_schema.index, MultiIndex) and append
1078	                else [new_schema.index]
1079	            )
1080	        )
1081	
1082	        for col in keys_temp:
1083	            ind_list.append(
1084	                Index(
1085	                    dtype=new_schema.columns[col].dtype,
1086	                    name=col,
1087	                    checks=new_schema.columns[col].checks,
1088	                    nullable=new_schema.columns[col].nullable,
1089	                    unique=new_schema.columns[col].unique,
1090	                    coerce=new_schema.columns[col].coerce,
1091	                    title=new_schema.columns[col].title,
1092	                    description=new_schema.columns[col].description,
1093	                    metadata=new_schema.columns[col].metadata,
1094	                )
1095	            )
1096	
1097	        new_schema.index = (
1098	            ind_list[0] if len(ind_list) == 1 else MultiIndex(ind_list)
1099	        )
1100	
1101	        # if drop is True as defaulted, drop the columns moved into the index
1102	        if drop:
1103	            new_schema = new_schema.remove_columns(keys_temp)
1104	
1105	        return cast(Self, new_schema)
1106	
1107	    def reset_index(
1108	        self, level: list[str] | None = None, drop: bool = False
1109	    ) -> Self:
1110	        """
1111	        A method for resetting the :class:`Index` of a
1112	        :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
1113	
1114	        :param level: list of labels
1115	        :param drop: bool, default True
1116	        :return: a new :class:`~pandera.api.dataframe.container.DataFrameSchema`
1117	            with specified column(s) in the index.
1118	        :raises: :class:`~pandera.errors.SchemaInitError` if no index set in
1119	            schema.
1120	        :examples:
1121	
1122	        Similar to the ``pandas`` reset_index method on a pandas DataFrame,
1123	        this method can be used to to fully or partially reset indices of a
1124	        schema.
1125	
1126	        To remove the entire index from the schema, just call the reset_index
1127	        method with default parameters.
1128	
1129	        >>> import pandera.pandas as pa
1130	        >>>
1131	        >>> example_schema = pa.DataFrameSchema(
1132	        ...     {"probability" : pa.Column(float)},
1133	        ...     index = pa.Index(name="unique_id", dtype=int)
1134	        ... )
1135	        >>>
1136	        >>> print(example_schema.reset_index())
1137	        <Schema DataFrameSchema(
1138	            columns={
1139	                'probability': <Schema Column(name=probability, type=DataType(float64))>
1140	                'unique_id': <Schema Column(name=unique_id, type=DataType(int64))>
1141	            },
1142	            checks=[],
1143	            parsers=[],
1144	            coerce=False,
1145	            dtype=None,
1146	            index=None,
1147	            strict=False,
1148	            name=None,
1149	            ordered=False,
1150	            unique_column_names=False,
1151	            metadata=None,
1152	            add_missing_columns=False
1153	        )>
1154	
1155	        This reclassifies an index (or indices) as a column (or columns).
1156	
1157	        Similarly, to partially alter the index, pass the name of the column
1158	        you would like to be removed to the ``level`` parameter, and you may
1159	        also decide whether to drop the levels with the ``drop`` parameter.
1160	
1161	        >>> example_schema = pa.DataFrameSchema({
1162	        ...     "category" : pa.Column(str)},
1163	        ...     index = pa.MultiIndex([
1164	        ...         pa.Index(name="unique_id1", dtype=int),
1165	        ...         pa.Index(name="unique_id2", dtype=str)
1166	        ...         ]
1167	        ...     )
1168	        ... )
1169	        >>> print(example_schema.reset_index(level = ["unique_id1"]))
1170	        <Schema DataFrameSchema(
1171	            columns={
1172	                'category': <Schema Column(name=category, type=DataType(str))>
1173	                'unique_id1': <Schema Column(name=unique_id1, type=DataType(int64))>
1174	            },
1175	            checks=[],
1176	            parsers=[],
1177	            coerce=False,
1178	            dtype=None,
1179	            index=<Schema Index(name=unique_id2, type=DataType(str))>,
1180	            strict=False,
1181	            name=None,
1182	            ordered=False,
1183	            unique_column_names=False,
1184	            metadata=None,
1185	            add_missing_columns=False
1186	        )>
1187	
1188	        .. seealso:: :func:`set_index`
1189	
1190	        """
1191	
1192	        from pandera.api.pandas.components import Column, Index, MultiIndex
1193	
1194	        # explicit check for an empty list
1195	        if level == []:
1196	            return self
1197	
1198	        new_schema = copy.deepcopy(self)
1199	
1200	        if new_schema.index is None:
1201	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
1202	                "There is currently no index set for this schema."
1203	            )
1204	
1205	        # ensure no duplicates
1206	        level_temp: Union[list[Any], list[str]] = (
1207	            new_schema.index.names if level is None else list(set(level))
1208	        )
1209	
1210	        # ensure all specified keys are present in the index
1211	        level_not_in_index: Union[list[Any], list[str], None] = (
1212	            [x for x in level_temp if x not in new_schema.index.names]
1213	            if isinstance(new_schema.index, MultiIndex) and level_temp
1214	            else (
1215	                []
1216	                if isinstance(new_schema.index, Index)
1217	                and (level_temp == [new_schema.index.name])
1218	                else level_temp
1219	            )
1220	        )
1221	        if level_not_in_index:
1222	            raise errors.SchemaInitError(
1223	                f"Keys {level_not_in_index} not found in schema columns!"
1224	            )
1225	
1226	        new_index = (
1227	            None
1228	            if not level_temp or isinstance(new_schema.index, Index)
1229	            else new_schema.index.remove_columns(level_temp)
1230	        )
1231	        new_index = (
1232	            new_index
1233	            if new_index is None
1234	            else (
1235	                Index(
1236	                    dtype=new_index.columns[list(new_index.columns)[0]].dtype,
1237	                    checks=new_index.columns[
1238	                        list(new_index.columns)[0]
1239	                    ].checks,
1240	                    nullable=new_index.columns[
1241	                        list(new_index.columns)[0]
1242	                    ].nullable,
1243	                    unique=new_index.columns[
1244	                        list(new_index.columns)[0]
1245	                    ].unique,
1246	                    coerce=new_index.columns[
1247	                        list(new_index.columns)[0]
1248	                    ].coerce,
1249	                    name=new_index.columns[list(new_index.columns)[0]].name,
1250	                )
1251	                if (len(list(new_index.columns)) == 1)
1252	                and (new_index is not None)
1253	                else (
1254	                    None
1255	                    if (len(list(new_index.columns)) == 0)
1256	                    and (new_index is not None)
1257	                    else new_index
1258	                )
1259	            )
1260	        )
1261	
1262	        if not drop:
1263	            additional_columns: dict[str, Any] = (
1264	                {col: new_schema.index.columns.get(col) for col in level_temp}
1265	                if isinstance(new_schema.index, MultiIndex)
1266	                else {new_schema.index.name: new_schema.index}
1267	            )
1268	            new_schema = new_schema.add_columns(
1269	                {
1270	                    k: Column(
1271	                        dtype=v.dtype,
1272	                        parsers=v.parsers,
1273	                        checks=v.checks,
1274	                        nullable=v.nullable,
1275	                        unique=v.unique,
1276	                        coerce=v.coerce,
1277	                        name=v.name,
1278	                    )
1279	                    for (k, v) in additional_columns.items()
1280	                }
1281	            )
1282	
1283	        new_schema.index = new_index
1284	
1285	        return new_schema
1286	
1287	
1288	def _validate_columns(
1289	    column_dict: dict[Any, Any],  # type: ignore [name-defined]
1290	) -> None:
1291	    for column_name, column in column_dict.items():
1292	        for check in column.checks:
1293	            if check.groupby is None or callable(check.groupby):
1294	                continue
1295	            nonexistent_groupby_columns = [
1296	                c for c in check.groupby if c not in column_dict
1297	            ]
1298	            if nonexistent_groupby_columns:
1299	                raise errors.SchemaInitError(
1300	                    f"groupby argument {nonexistent_groupby_columns} in "
1301	                    f"Check for Column {column_name} not "
1302	                    "specified in the DataFrameSchema."
1303	                )
1304	
1305	
1306	def _columns_renamed(
1307	    columns: dict[Any, Any],  # type: ignore [name-defined]
1308	) -> dict[Any, Any]:  # type: ignore [name-defined]
1309	    def renamed(column, new_name):
1310	        column = copy.deepcopy(column)
1311	        column.set_name(new_name)
1312	        return column
1313	
1314	    return {
1315	        column_name: renamed(column, column_name)
1316	        for column_name, column in columns.items()
1317	    }

1	"""Class-based API for pandas models."""
2	
3	import sys
4	from typing import Any, Optional, Union, cast
5	
6	import pandas as pd
7	
8	from pandera.api.base.schema import BaseSchema
9	from pandera.api.checks import Check
10	from pandera.api.dataframe.model import DataFrameModel as _DataFrameModel
11	from pandera.api.dataframe.model import get_dtype_kwargs
12	from pandera.api.dataframe.model_components import FieldInfo
13	from pandera.api.pandas.components import Column, Index, MultiIndex
14	from pandera.api.pandas.container import DataFrameSchema
15	from pandera.api.pandas.model_config import BaseConfig
16	from pandera.api.parsers import Parser
17	from pandera.engines import numpy_engine, pandas_engine
18	from pandera.engines.pandas_engine import Engine
19	from pandera.errors import SchemaInitError
20	from pandera.typing import (
21	    AnnotationInfo,
22	    DataFrame,
23	    get_index_types,
24	    get_series_types,
25	)
26	from pandera.utils import docstring_substitution
27	
28	# if python version is < 3.11, import Self from typing_extensions
29	if sys.version_info < (3, 11):
30	    from typing_extensions import Self
31	else:
32	    from typing import Self
33	
34	SchemaIndex = Union[Index, MultiIndex]
35	
36	
37	def _get_nullable_coercion_dtype(
38	    dtype: Any,
39	    *,
40	    nullable: bool,
41	    coerce: bool,
42	) -> Any:
43	    """Use nullable pandas dtypes for nullable fields when coercing."""
44	    if dtype is None or not nullable or not coerce:
45	        return dtype
46	
47	    try:
48	        pandera_dtype = Engine.dtype(dtype)
49	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
50	        return dtype
51	
52	    nullable_dtype_map = {
53	        numpy_engine.Bool: pandas_engine.BOOL,
54	        numpy_engine.Int8: pandas_engine.INT8,
55	        numpy_engine.Int16: pandas_engine.INT16,
56	        numpy_engine.Int32: pandas_engine.INT32,
57	        numpy_engine.Int64: pandas_engine.INT64,
58	        numpy_engine.UInt8: pandas_engine.UINT8,
59	        numpy_engine.UInt16: pandas_engine.UINT16,
60	        numpy_engine.UInt32: pandas_engine.UINT32,
61	        numpy_engine.UInt64: pandas_engine.UINT64,
62	    }
63	
64	    for numpy_dtype, nullable_dtype in nullable_dtype_map.items():
65	        if isinstance(pandera_dtype, numpy_dtype):
66	            return nullable_dtype
67	
68	    return dtype
69	
70	
71	class DataFrameModel(_DataFrameModel[pd.DataFrame, DataFrameSchema]):
72	    """Model of a pandas :class:`~pandera.api.pandas.container.DataFrameSchema`.
73	
74	    *new in 0.5.0*
75	
76	    See the :ref:`User Guide <dataframe-models>` for more.
77	    """
78	
79	    Config: type[BaseConfig] = BaseConfig
80	
81	    @classmethod
82	    def build_schema_(cls, **kwargs) -> DataFrameSchema:
83	        multiindex_kwargs = {
84	            name[len("multiindex_") :]: value
85	            for name, value in vars(cls.__config__).items()
86	            if name.startswith("multiindex_")
87	        }
88	        columns, index = cls._build_columns_index(
89	            cls.__fields__,
90	            cls.__checks__,
91	            cls.__parsers__,
92	            **multiindex_kwargs,
93	        )
94	        return DataFrameSchema(
95	            columns,
96	            index=index,
97	            checks=cls.__root_checks__,
98	            parsers=cls.__root_parsers__,
99	            **kwargs,
100	        )
101	
102	    @classmethod
103	    def _build_columns_index(
104	        cls,
105	        fields: dict[str, tuple[AnnotationInfo, FieldInfo]],
106	        checks: dict[str, list[Check]],
107	        parsers: dict[str, list[Parser]],
108	        **multiindex_kwargs: Any,
109	    ) -> tuple[
110	        dict[str, Column],
111	        Union[Index, MultiIndex] | None,
112	    ]:
113	        index_count = sum(
114	            annotation.origin in get_index_types()
115	            for annotation, _ in fields.values()
116	        )
117	
118	        columns: dict[str, Column] = {}
119	        indices: list[Index] = []
120	
121	        for field_name, (annotation, field) in fields.items():
122	            field_checks = checks.get(field_name, [])
123	            field_parsers = parsers.get(field_name, [])
124	            field_name = field.name
125	            check_name = getattr(field, "check_name", None)
126	
127	            use_raw_annotation = False
128	            if annotation.metadata:
129	                if field.dtype_kwargs:
130	                    raise TypeError(
131	                        "Cannot specify redundant 'dtype_kwargs' "
132	                        + f"for {annotation.raw_annotation}."
133	                        + "\n Usage Tip: Drop 'typing.Annotated'."
134	                    )
135	                dtype_kwargs = get_dtype_kwargs(annotation)
136	                dtype = annotation.arg(**dtype_kwargs)  # type: ignore
137	            elif annotation.default_dtype:
138	                dtype = annotation.default_dtype
139	            else:
140	                try:
141	                    # if the raw annotation is accepted by the engine, use it as
142	                    # the dtype
143	                    Engine.dtype(annotation.raw_annotation)
144	                    dtype = annotation.raw_annotation
145	                    use_raw_annotation = True
146	                except (TypeError, ValueError):
147	                    dtype = annotation.arg
148	
149	            dtype = None if dtype is Any else dtype
150	
151	            if (
152	                annotation.is_annotated_type
153	                or annotation.origin is None
154	                or use_raw_annotation
155	                or annotation.origin in get_series_types()
156	                or annotation.raw_annotation in get_series_types()
157	            ):
158	                if check_name is False:
159	                    raise SchemaInitError(
160	                        f"'check_name' is not supported for {field_name}."
161	                    )
162	
163	                dtype = _get_nullable_coercion_dtype(
164	                    dtype,
165	                    nullable=field.nullable,
166	                    coerce=field.coerce
167	                    or bool(getattr(cls.__config__, "coerce", False)),
168	                )
169	
170	                column_kwargs = (
171	                    field.column_properties(
172	                        dtype,
173	                        required=not annotation.optional,
174	                        checks=field_checks,
175	                        parsers=field_parsers,
176	                        name=field_name,
177	                    )
178	                    if field
179	                    else {}
180	                )
181	                columns[field_name] = Column(**column_kwargs)
182	
183	            elif (
184	                annotation.origin in get_index_types()
185	                or annotation.raw_annotation in get_index_types()
186	            ):
187	                if annotation.optional:
188	                    raise SchemaInitError(
189	                        f"Index '{field_name}' cannot be Optional."
190	                    )
191	
192	                if check_name is False or (
193	                    # default single index
194	                    check_name is None and index_count == 1
195	                ):
196	                    field_name = None  # type:ignore
197	
198	                index_kwargs = (
199	                    field.index_properties(
200	                        dtype,
201	                        checks=field_checks,
202	                        name=field_name,
203	                    )
204	                    if field
205	                    else {}
206	                )
207	                index = Index(**index_kwargs)
208	                indices.append(index)
209	            else:
210	                raise SchemaInitError(
211	                    f"Invalid annotation '{field_name}: "
212	                    f"{annotation.raw_annotation}'"
213	                )
214	
215	        return columns, _build_schema_index(indices, **multiindex_kwargs)
216	
217	    @classmethod
218	    @docstring_substitution(validate_doc=BaseSchema.validate.__doc__)
219	    def validate(
220	        cls: type[Self],
221	        check_obj: pd.DataFrame,
222	        head: int | None = None,
223	        tail: int | None = None,
224	        sample: int | None = None,
225	        random_state: int | None = None,
226	        lazy: bool = False,
227	        inplace: bool = False,
228	    ) -> DataFrame[Self]:
229	        """%(validate_doc)s"""
230	        return cast(
231	            DataFrame[Self],
232	            cls.to_schema().validate(
233	                check_obj, head, tail, sample, random_state, lazy, inplace
234	            ),
235	        )
236	
237	    @classmethod
238	    def to_json_schema(cls):
239	        """Serialize schema metadata into json-schema format.
240	
241	        :param dataframe_schema: schema to write to json-schema format.
242	
243	        .. note::
244	
245	            This function is currently does not fully specify a pandera schema,
246	            and is primarily used internally to render OpenAPI docs via the
247	            FastAPI integration.
248	        """
249	        schema = cls.to_schema()
250	        empty = pd.DataFrame(columns=schema.columns.keys()).astype(
251	            {k: v.type if v else None for k, v in schema.dtypes.items()}
252	        )
253	        table_schema = pd.io.json.build_table_schema(empty)
254	
255	        def _field_json_schema(field):
256	            return {
257	                "type": "array",
258	                "items": {
259	                    "type": (
260	                        field["type"]
261	                        if field["type"] != "any" or "extDtype" not in field
262	                        else field["extDtype"]
263	                    )
264	                },
265	            }
266	
267	        return {
268	            "title": schema.name or "pandera.DataFrameSchema",
269	            "type": "object",
270	            "properties": {
271	                field["name"]: _field_json_schema(field)
272	                for field in table_schema["fields"]
273	            },
274	        }
275	
276	    @classmethod
277	    def empty(cls: type[Self], *_args) -> DataFrame[Self]:
278	        """Create an empty DataFrame with the schema of this model."""
279	        schema = cls.to_schema()
280	
281	        data = {}
282	        for col_name, col_schema in schema.columns.items():
283	            if col_schema.dtype is not None:
284	                data[col_name] = pd.array([], dtype=col_schema.dtype.type)
285	            else:
286	                data[col_name] = pd.array([])
287	
288	        if isinstance(schema.index, MultiIndex):
289	            index = pd.MultiIndex.from_arrays(
290	                [
291	                    pd.Index(
292	                        [],
293	                        dtype=idx.dtype.type
294	                        if idx.dtype is not None
295	                        else None,
296	                        name=idx.name,
297	                    )
298	                    for idx in schema.index.indexes
299	                ]
300	            )
301	        elif isinstance(schema.index, Index):
302	            index = pd.Index(
303	                [],
304	                dtype=schema.index.dtype.type
305	                if schema.index.dtype is not None
306	                else None,
307	                name=schema.index.name,
308	            )
309	        else:
310	            index = None
311	
312	        empty_df = pd.DataFrame(data, index=index)
313	        return DataFrame[Self](empty_df)
314	
315	
316	def _build_schema_index(
317	    indices: list[Index], **multiindex_kwargs: Any
318	) -> SchemaIndex | None:
319	    index: SchemaIndex | None = None
320	    if indices:
321	        if len(indices) == 1:
322	            index = indices[0]
323	        else:
324	            index = MultiIndex(indices, **multiindex_kwargs)
325	    return index
326

1	"""Module for inferring dataframe/series schema."""
2	
3	from typing import overload
4	
5	import pandas as pd
6	
7	from pandera.api.pandas.array import SeriesSchema
8	from pandera.api.pandas.components import Column, Index, MultiIndex
9	from pandera.api.pandas.container import DataFrameSchema
10	from pandera.schema_statistics.pandas import (
11	    infer_dataframe_statistics,
12	    infer_series_statistics,
13	    parse_check_statistics,
14	)
15	
16	
17	@overload
18	def infer_schema(
19	    pandas_obj: pd.Series,

31	def infer_schema(pandas_obj):
32	    """Infer schema for pandas DataFrame or Series object.
33	
34	    :param pandas_obj: DataFrame or Series object to infer.
35	    :returns: DataFrameSchema or SeriesSchema
36	    :raises: TypeError if pandas_obj is not expected type.
37	    """
38	    if isinstance(pandas_obj, pd.DataFrame):
39	        return infer_dataframe_schema(pandas_obj)
40	    elif isinstance(pandas_obj, pd.Series):
41	        return infer_series_schema(pandas_obj)
42	    else:
43	        raise TypeError(
44	            "pandas_obj type not recognized. Expected a pandas DataFrame or "
45	            f"Series, found {type(pandas_obj)}"
46	        )
47	
48	
49	def _create_index(index_statistics):
50	    index = [
51	        Index(
52	            properties["dtype"],
53	            checks=parse_check_statistics(properties["checks"]),
54	            nullable=properties["nullable"],
55	            name=properties["name"],
56	        )
57	        for properties in index_statistics
58	    ]
59	    if len(index) == 1:
60	        index = index[0]  # type: ignore
61	    else:
62	        index = MultiIndex(index)  # type: ignore
63	
64	    return index
65	
66	
67	def infer_dataframe_schema(df: pd.DataFrame) -> DataFrameSchema:
68	    """Infer a DataFrameSchema from a pandas DataFrame.
69	
70	    :param df: DataFrame object to infer.
71	    :returns: DataFrameSchema
72	    """
73	    df_statistics = infer_dataframe_statistics(df)
74	    schema = DataFrameSchema(
75	        columns={
76	            colname: Column(
77	                properties["dtype"],
78	                checks=parse_check_statistics(properties["checks"]),
79	                nullable=properties["nullable"],
80	            )
81	            for colname, properties in df_statistics["columns"].items()
82	        },
83	        index=_create_index(df_statistics["index"]),
84	        coerce=True,
85	    )
86	    return schema
87	
88	
89	def infer_series_schema(series) -> SeriesSchema:
90	    """Infer a SeriesSchema from a pandas DataFrame.
91	
92	    :param series: Series object to infer.
93	    :returns: SeriesSchema
94	    """
95	    series_statistics = infer_series_statistics(series)
96	    schema = SeriesSchema(
97	        dtype=series_statistics["dtype"],
98	        checks=parse_check_statistics(series_statistics["checks"]),
99	        nullable=series_statistics["nullable"],
100	        name=series_statistics["name"],
101	        coerce=True,
102	    )
103	    return schema

import copy
import inspect
import os

import threading
import typing
from collections.abc import Iterable
from typing import (
    Any,
    ClassVar,

)

from typing_extensions import Self

from pandera.api.base.schema import BaseSchema
from pandera.api.checks import Check
from pandera.api.parsers import Parser
from pandera.engines import PYDANTIC_V2
from pandera.errors import SchemaInitError
from pandera.import_utils import strategy_import_error
from pandera.typing import AnnotationInfo
from pandera.typing.common import DataFrameBase
from pandera.utils import docstring_substitution

from .model_components import (
    CHECK_KEY,
    DATAFRAME_CHECK_KEY,
    DATAFRAME_PARSER_KEY,
    PARSER_KEY,
    CheckInfo,
    Field,
    FieldCheckInfo,
    FieldInfo,
    FieldParserInfo,
    ParserInfo,
)
from .model_config import BaseConfig

if PYDANTIC_V2:
    from pydantic import GetCoreSchemaHandler, GetJsonSchemaHandler
    from pydantic_core import core_schema


TDataFrame = TypeVar("TDataFrame")
TSchema = TypeVar("TSchema", bound=BaseSchema)
TFields = dict[str, tuple[AnnotationInfo, FieldInfo]]
TChecks = dict[str, list[Check]]
TParsers = dict[str, list[Parser]]

_CONFIG_KEY = "Config"
MODEL_CACHE: dict[tuple[type["DataFrameModel"], int], Any] = {}
GENERIC_SCHEMA_CACHE: dict[
    tuple[type["DataFrameModel"], tuple[type[Any], ...]],
    type["DataFrameModel"],
] = {}


def get_dtype_kwargs(annotation: AnnotationInfo) -> dict[str, Any]:
    sig = inspect.signature(annotation.arg)  # type: ignore
    dtype_arg_names = list(sig.parameters.keys())
    if len(annotation.metadata) != len(dtype_arg_names):  # type: ignore
        raise TypeError(
            f"Annotation '{annotation.arg.__name__}' requires "  # type: ignore
            + f"all positional arguments {dtype_arg_names}."
        )
    return dict(zip(dtype_arg_names, annotation.metadata))  # type: ignore


def _is_field(name: str) -> bool:
    """Ignore private and reserved keywords."""
    return not name.startswith("_") and name != _CONFIG_KEY

    method.

        Parser.register_backend(t, PandasParserBackend)

162	def is_table(obj):
163	    """Verifies whether an object is table-like.
164	
165	    Where a table is a 2-dimensional data matrix of rows and columns, which
166	    can be indexed in multiple different ways.

181	    return backend_types is not None and isinstance(

"""Core geopandas dataframe container specification."""

import sys
from typing import Any, Union

import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd

if sys.version_info < (3, 11):
    from typing_extensions import Self
else:
    from typing import Self

from pandera.api.pandas.container import DataFrameSchema as PandasDataFrameSchema


def _active_geometry_name(gdf: gpd.GeoDataFrame) -> str | None:
    """Get the name of a GeoDataFrame's active geometry column.

    Newer versions of geopandas expose ``active_geometry_name``; older
    versions only have the private ``_geometry_column_name`` attribute.
    """
    return getattr(
        gdf, "active_geometry_name", None
    ) or gdf._geometry_column_name


class GeoDataFrameSchema(PandasDataFrameSchema):
    """A lightweight geopandas GeoDataFrame validator.

    This schema rides on the pandas validation backend and engine -- it
    does not define its own -- but guarantees that :func:`validate` always
    returns a :class:`geopandas.GeoDataFrame` with its geometry dtype and
    CRS intact, even when validating a plain :class:`pandas.DataFrame`
    (e.g. one containing WKT strings that get coerced into geometries).
    """

    @classmethod
    def get_backend(cls, check_obj=None, check_type=None):
        """Get the pandas backend associated with the type of ``check_obj``.

        ``get_backend`` is a classmethod, so calling it as ``self.get_backend``
        binds ``cls`` to ``GeoDataFrameSchema`` (the most-derived class),
        which has no backend registered under its own name -- only
        :class:`~pandera.api.pandas.container.DataFrameSchema` does.
        Delegate explicitly to the pandas schema class so the existing
        pandas backend registry is used, without duplicating any
        registration logic.
        """
        return PandasDataFrameSchema.get_backend(
            check_obj=check_obj, check_type=check_type
        )

    def validate(
        self,
        check_obj: Union[pd.DataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame],
        head: int | None = None,
        tail: int | None = None,
        sample: int | None = None,
        random_state: int | None = None,
        lazy: bool = False,
        inplace: bool = False,
    ) -> gpd.GeoDataFrame:
        """Validate a GeoDataFrame based on the schema specification.

        This delegates to the pandas validation backend and then guarantees
        that the returned object is a :class:`geopandas.GeoDataFrame` with
        the active geometry column and CRS preserved from the input (or
        inferred, if the input/output was a plain DataFrame).

        :param check_obj: the dataframe to be validated.
        :param head: validate the first n rows.
        :param tail: validate the last n rows.
        :param sample: validate a random sample of n rows.
        :param random_state: random seed for the ``sample`` argument.
        :param lazy: if True, lazily evaluates dataframe against all
            validation checks and raises a ``SchemaErrors``. Otherwise,
            raise ``SchemaError`` as soon as one occurs.
        :param inplace: if True, applies coercion to the object of
            validation, otherwise creates a copy of the data.
        :returns: validated :class:`geopandas.GeoDataFrame`
        :raises SchemaError: when the object violates built-in or custom
            checks.
        """
        validated = super().validate(
            check_obj, head, tail, sample, random_state, lazy, inplace
        )
        return self._ensure_geodataframe(validated, check_obj)

    @staticmethod
    def _ensure_geodataframe(
        validated: Union[pd.DataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame],
        original: Any,
    ) -> gpd.GeoDataFrame:
        """Guarantee that ``validated`` is a GeoDataFrame with geometry
        dtype and CRS intact, using ``original`` (the pre-validation input)
        to recover the active geometry column name and CRS when needed.
        """
        geometry_name: str | None = None
        crs = None
        if isinstance(original, gpd.GeoDataFrame):
            geometry_name = _active_geometry_name(original)
            crs = original.crs

        if isinstance(validated, gpd.GeoDataFrame):
            # Backend already returned a GeoDataFrame: avoid unnecessary
            # copies/reconstruction.
            result = validated
        else:
            # Plain DataFrame result (e.g. after coercion): reuse the
            # geometry-coercion logic from pandera.typing.geopandas so we
            # don't invent a second set of conversion rules.
            from pandera.typing.geopandas import (
                GeoDataFrame as _PanderaGeoDataFrame,
            )

            result = _PanderaGeoDataFrame._coerce_geometry(validated)
            if not isinstance(result, gpd.GeoDataFrame):
                result = gpd.GeoDataFrame(result)

        result = GeoDataFrameSchema._restore_active_geometry(
            result, geometry_name
        )
        result = GeoDataFrameSchema._restore_crs(result, crs)
        return result

    @staticmethod
    def _restore_active_geometry(
        gdf: gpd.GeoDataFrame, geometry_name: str | None
    ) -> gpd.GeoDataFrame:
        """Ensure ``gdf`` has an active geometry column set.

        Prefers ``geometry_name`` (the input's active geometry column) when
        present. Otherwise, falls back to a column literally named
        "geometry", or the single geometry-dtyped column if there is
        exactly one.
        """
        current = _active_geometry_name(gdf)

        if geometry_name is not None and geometry_name in gdf.columns:
            if current != geometry_name:
                gdf = gdf.set_geometry(geometry_name)
            return gdf

        if current is not None:
            # Already has an active geometry column; nothing more to do.
            return gdf

        geometry_dtyped_cols = [
            col for col in gdf.columns if str(gdf[col].dtype) == "geometry"
        ]
        if "geometry" in geometry_dtyped_cols:
            gdf = gdf.set_geometry("geometry")
        elif len(geometry_dtyped_cols) == 1:
            gdf = gdf.set_geometry(geometry_dtyped_cols[0])

        return gdf

    @staticmethod
    def _restore_crs(gdf: gpd.GeoDataFrame, crs: Any) -> gpd.GeoDataFrame:
        """If the input had a CRS but the output's active geometry column
        does not, restore it. A CRS declared on a schema column via
        ``Geometry(crs=...)`` is otherwise applied during coercion by the
        engine, so this only fills in the gap left by non-coercing
        validation of a GeoDataFrame that already carries a CRS.
        """
        if crs is None:
            return gdf
        active_name = _active_geometry_name(gdf)
        if active_name is not None and gdf[active_name].crs is None:
            gdf[active_name] = gdf[active_name].set_crs(crs)
        return gdf

    @classmethod
    def _from_pandas_schema(cls, schema: PandasDataFrameSchema) -> Self:
        """Convert a plain pandas :class:`DataFrameSchema` into a
        :class:`GeoDataFrameSchema`, passing through all constructor
        attributes.
        """
        return cls(
            columns=schema.columns,
            checks=schema.checks,
            parsers=schema.parsers,
            index=schema.index,
            dtype=schema.dtype,
            coerce=schema.coerce,
            strict=schema.strict,
            name=schema.name,
            ordered=schema.ordered,
            unique=schema.unique,
            report_duplicates=schema.report_duplicates,
            unique_column_names=schema.unique_column_names,
            add_missing_columns=schema.add_missing_columns,
            title=schema.title,
            description=schema.description,
            metadata=schema.metadata,
            drop_invalid_rows=schema.drop_invalid_rows,
        )

    @classmethod
    def from_json(cls, source) -> Self:
        """Load schema from JSON (see :mod:`pandera.io.pandas_io`)."""
        from pandera.io import pandas_io

        return cls._from_pandas_schema(pandas_io.from_json(source))

    @classmethod
    def from_yaml(cls, yaml_schema) -> Self:
        """Load schema from YAML (see :mod:`pandera.io.pandas_io`)."""
        from pandera.io import pandas_io

        return cls._from_pandas_schema(pandas_io.from_yaml(yaml_schema))

"""Class-based API for geopandas models."""

from pandera.api.geopandas.container import GeoDataFrameSchema
from pandera.api.pandas.model import DataFrameModel as PandasDataFrameModel
from pandera.api.pandas.model_config import BaseConfig


class GeoDataFrameModel(PandasDataFrameModel):
    """Model of a :class:`~pandera.api.geopandas.container.GeoDataFrameSchema`.

    See the :ref:`User Guide <dataframe-models>` for more, and
    :class:`~pandera.api.geopandas.container.GeoDataFrameSchema` for the
    guarantees this model's ``validate`` provides (namely, that it always
    returns a :class:`geopandas.GeoDataFrame`).
    """

    Config: type[BaseConfig] = BaseConfig

    @classmethod
    def build_schema_(cls, **kwargs) -> GeoDataFrameSchema:
        pandas_schema = super().build_schema_(**kwargs)
        return GeoDataFrameSchema._from_pandas_schema(pandas_schema)

"""Module for inferring geopandas GeoDataFrame schema."""

import geopandas as gpd

from pandera.api.geopandas.container import GeoDataFrameSchema
from pandera.schema_inference.pandas import infer_dataframe_schema


def infer_schema(gpd_obj: gpd.GeoDataFrame) -> GeoDataFrameSchema:
    """Infer schema for a geopandas GeoDataFrame object.

    :param gpd_obj: GeoDataFrame object to infer.
    :returns: GeoDataFrameSchema
    :raises: TypeError if gpd_obj is not expected type.
    """
    if isinstance(gpd_obj, gpd.GeoDataFrame):
        pandas_schema = infer_dataframe_schema(gpd_obj)
        return GeoDataFrameSchema._from_pandas_schema(pandas_schema)
    else:
        raise TypeError(
            "gpd_obj type not recognized. Expected a geopandas GeoDataFrame, "
            f"found {type(gpd_obj)}"
        )
